areas.name: Patient Chart And Registration
areas.summary: Core patient lookup, protected and ACL-authorized chart access, chart summary, duplicate detection, registration validation, deceased-status administration, guardian-contact/detail administration, social-detail administration, primary-provider assignment, provider and contact-backed care-team membership, patient history/lifestyle, insurance subscriber details, portal account/reset/access/authentication/session/home/profile demographics and insurance plus home immunization health-snapshot/clinical-summary problem, allergy, and medication date-column plus prescription modified-date/start-date/end-date filtering/lab-results/medical-report/appointments/appointment-request/secure-message inbox/compose/reply/thread-view/archive/read-status/mark-all-read/batch-archive/all-folder/pagination readiness, portal generated-report issue selection, encounter-form selection, procedure-order selection, procedure-order delivery artifacts, printable template metadata, and report package delivery, demographics, contact, registration, insurance, and immunization workflows have parity coverage, with richer administrative paths still open.

completed.label: Patient search and chart summary
completed.detail: React chart workspace, patient APIs, PostgreSQL seed mapping, and side-by-side patient-readiness parity are implemented.

completed.label: Patient demographic/contact mutation
completed.detail: Contact update, demographics update/restore, and temporary registration lifecycle behavior are covered.

completed.label: Insurance and immunization coverage
completed.detail: Read and mutation paths for insurance plus pediatric immunization history and entered-in-error workflows are covered.

completed.label: Patient chart protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient search/chart API now requires an active session, the Patient/Client page gates search and chart data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy patient-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Patient chart authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient search/chart API now enforces mirrored Demographics ACL access, accepts both admin and front-desk demo sessions through seeded write grants, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient mutation API now distinguishes Demographics add-only registration access from write-level existing chart updates, contact edits, insurance edits, and temporary patient deletion.

completed.label: Patient duplicate detection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client page now surface candidate duplicates from first name, last name, date of birth, phone, and email matches, with parity coverage that creates and cleans up temporary duplicate registrations.

completed.label: Patient registration validation readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient registration API now mirrors OpenEMR PatientValidator rules for required identity fields, DOB format, sex, last-name length, and email syntax, returns structured validation details, renders them in the Patient/Client form, and proves invalid drafts create no row.

completed.label: Patient deceased status readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API, Patient/Client chart, PostgreSQL seed schema, and parity harness now mirror OpenEMR deceased date and reason fields with restore-backed mutation coverage.

completed.label: Patient guardian contact readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API, Patient/Client chart, shared gold dataset, and parity harness now mirror OpenEMR mother and guardian contact fields with restore-backed mutation coverage.

completed.label: Patient guardian demographic/address readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API, Patient/Client chart, shared gold dataset, and parity harness now mirror OpenEMR guardian sex, address, country, and work-phone fields with restore-backed mutation coverage.

completed.label: Patient social detail readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API, Patient/Client chart, shared gold dataset, and parity harness now mirror OpenEMR race, ethnicity, interpreter, family size, monthly income, homeless, and financial-review fields with restore-backed mutation coverage.

completed.label: Patient employer core readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API, Patient/Client chart, PostgreSQL employer table, shared gold dataset, and parity harness now mirror OpenEMR employer identity and address fields with restore-backed mutation coverage.

completed.label: Patient provider assignment readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client chart now mirror OpenEMR primary provider assignment through provider-option menus, ID/name projection, and restore-backed parity coverage.

completed.label: Patient care-team membership readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client chart now mirror OpenEMR care-team lead-member assignment through care-team tables, provider/facility projection, and restore-backed parity coverage.

completed.label: Patient multi-member care-team readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client chart now update and render multiple OpenEMR-style care-team members through shared provider/facility projection and restore-backed parity coverage.

completed.label: Patient contact-backed care-team readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client chart now update and render patient-contact care-team members through deterministic related-contact seed rows and restore-backed parity coverage.

completed.label: Patient history and lifestyle readiness
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset now seeds one OpenEMR History and Lifestyle row per patient, the modernized API and Patient/Client chart project the same read-only history fields, and parity verifies database/API/UI behavior.

completed.label: Patient insurance subscriber readiness
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset, legacy seed, modernized PostgreSQL seed, APIs, Patient/Client insurance panel, and parity harness now preserve and mutate OpenEMR subscriber identity, address, phone, sex, date-of-birth, relationship, and employer fields.

completed.label: Patient portal account readiness
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset now provisions OpenEMR onsite portal-account rows for portal-enabled patients, the modernized PostgreSQL seed/API/Patient chart mirrors the same CMS login and account status facts, and parity verifies database/API/UI behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal reset readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client Portal Account panel now issue and clear one-time portal reset state while parity mutates/restores the matching legacy `patient_access_onsite` fields.

completed.label: Patient portal access readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient API and Patient/Client Portal Account panel now revoke and grant portal access while preserving provisioned account facts, and parity mutates/restores legacy `patient_data.allow_patient_portal`.

completed.label: Patient portal authentication readiness
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset now includes a deterministic synthetic portal credential, the modernized patient portal API issues patient-session readiness responses, and parity verifies valid login plus invalid, disabled, and pending-reset rejection paths.

completed.label: Patient portal session readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API now ends portal sessions, the Patient/Client Portal Account panel exposes an end-session command after readiness sign-in, and parity verifies active-session read, logout, and reuse rejection against legacy portal logout behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal home readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now open a session-protected home summary with identity, message counts, and upcoming appointments for the signed-in portal patient, with parity anchored to MOD-PAT-0004.

completed.label: Patient portal home immunization readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal home API and Portal page now render the legacy health-snapshot immunization list, including entered-in-error rows that legacy OpenEMR's portal home still displays.

completed.label: Patient portal profile readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal profile API and Portal page now render legacy-style medical-record demographics plus primary and secondary insurance facts for the signed-in patient.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message readiness
completed.detail: The legacy gold seed now projects portal message anchors into OpenEMR onsite_mail, the modernized patient portal API and Portal page render the signed-in patient's secure-message inbox, and parity verifies MOD-PAT-0004 message detail on both portal surfaces.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message compose readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now compose secure messages into mailbox-style sent and recipient copies, while parity verifies the same cleanup-backed sent-message behavior against legacy onsite_mail.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message reply readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now reply to seeded inbox messages with mailbox thread linkage, while parity verifies cleanup-backed reply and sent-folder behavior against legacy onsite_mail.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message thread view readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now load patient-visible secure-message conversation threads around seeded inbox messages, while parity compares chronological thread rows against legacy onsite_mail after a cleanup-backed reply.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message archive readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now archive patient-visible secure messages using the legacy Delete status and soft-delete visibility rules, while parity verifies active-folder removal and archive visibility with cleanup-backed temporary messages.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message read-status readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now mark patient-visible secure messages read while preserving inbox visibility, and parity verifies legacy New-to-Read behavior with cleanup-backed temporary inbound messages.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message mark-all-read readiness
completed.detail: The modernized Portal now mirrors legacy OpenEMR's toolbar mark-all-read behavior as a browser-visible status change that is cleared on refresh rather than persisted to the mailbox row.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message batch archive readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now archive selected patient-visible secure messages together through legacy Delete-status soft-delete semantics, while parity verifies multi-message active-folder removal through database and UI surfaces.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message All-folder readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now expose the legacy active All-folder mailbox view, while parity verifies temporary inbound and sent message inclusion plus archived-row exclusion for the signed-in patient.

completed.label: Patient portal document list/download readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now list active patient-owned documents by legacy category and build a selected-document ZIP download, while parity compares the same list and download package behavior against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment list readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now list signed-in patient upcoming and past appointment cards with provider, facility, status, and category details, while parity compares the same future/past portal appointment behavior against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment request readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now let a signed-in patient request a pending appointment and create the matching provider reminder note, while parity compares the same portal scheduling request behavior against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment request options readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now derive appointment request visit, provider, facility, duration, and default selections from target data instead of hard-coded form IDs, while parity compares the same option readiness against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal clinical summary readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now render signed-in patient problems, allergies, medication-list entries, and active prescriptions, while parity compares the same portal clinical summary facts against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal prescription modified-date readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal clinical-summary API and Portal page now preserve and render active prescription modified dates in line with legacy OpenEMR's portal prescription table.

completed.label: Patient portal prescription start-date readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal clinical-summary API and Portal page now preserve and render active prescription date-added timestamps as portal start dates in line with legacy OpenEMR's portal prescription table.

completed.label: Patient portal prescription end-date filtering readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal clinical-summary API and Portal page now keep ended prescriptions out of the active prescription list while preserving null end dates for active prescription cards.

completed.label: Patient portal lab results readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now render signed-in patient lab orders, reports, and final result rows, while parity compares the same portal lab-results facts against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal medical report readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now render customized medical-history report sections, issue choices, encounter/form choices, procedure-order choices, and preview facts while parity compares the same report-builder readiness against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now generate the default customized medical-history report content with patient data, billing rollup, and selected procedure-order lines while parity compares the same generated report behavior against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report PDF readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now export the generated customized medical-history report as a deterministic PDF while parity compares legacy portal PDF POST behavior and modernized PDF download behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report issue selection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now include selected issue rows in generated customized medical-history reports while parity compares legacy issue-checkbox POST behavior and modernized issue ID and checkbox behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report encounter-form selection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now include selected encounter form rows in generated customized medical-history reports while parity compares legacy encounter/form checkbox POST behavior and modernized encounter form ID and checkbox behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report procedure-order selection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal generated-report panel now surfaces selected procedure-order count evidence while parity creates a cleanup-backed temporary order and compares explicit procedure-order inclusion against legacy OpenEMR.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report procedure-order artifact readiness
completed.detail: Selected procedure orders now carry into generated medical-report PDF and ZIP package artifacts while parity compares legacy source printable/PDF artifacts with modernized PDF, manifest, summary, and package PDF contents.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report printable template readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API, Portal page, and PDF payload now expose printable generated-report facility, patient header, generated-on, and signature-line metadata while parity compares the same legacy printable report header behavior.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report package readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API and Portal page now expose and download a generated medical-report ZIP package containing manifest, PDF, and summary entries while parity compares legacy source report artifacts and modernized package delivery.

completed.label: Patient portal generated medical report lifecycle audit readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal API now records generated-report, PDF-download, and package-download audit events, exposes a session-protected audit history endpoint, renders the report audit timeline in the Portal page, and parity compares normalized lifecycle evidence against the legacy generated-report source actions.

outstanding: Patient merge and broader guarantor insurance-party workflows.
Address validation catalogs, broader lifecycle audit trail, and broader patient administration policy depth.
deferred: External vaccine registry submission, forecast rules, inventory coupling, and broader immunization registry workflows.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Broad core workflow surface, but several patient-administration details are smaller than billing, encounters, and labs.
areas.estimateRationale: Strong chart, protected and ACL-authorized patient access, mutation-specific patient authorization, duplicate detection, registration validation, deceased-status administration, guardian-contact/detail administration, social-detail administration, primary-provider assignment, provider and contact-backed care-team membership, patient history/lifestyle, insurance subscriber details, portal account/reset/access/authentication/session/home/clinical-summary problem, allergy, and medication date-column plus prescription modified-date/start-date/end-date filtering/lab-results/medical-report/generated-report/PDF export/issue selection/encounter-form selection/procedure-order selection/procedure-order artifact delivery/printable template metadata/report package delivery/report lifecycle audit/appointments/appointment-request/request-options/secure-message inbox/compose/reply/thread-view/archive/read-status/mark-all-read/batch-archive/all-folder/pagination readiness, portal document list/download readiness, demographics, insurance, registration, and immunization coverage; merge, broader guarantor workflows, address validation, broader lifecycle audit, and broader administration depth remain.

areas.name: Scheduling
areas.summary: Appointment read, portal appointment list/request/request-options, lifecycle, recurrence, reassignment, status, overlap, reminder-readiness, protected schedule access, ACL-authorized front-desk schedule access, and mutation-specific Appointment write authorization are heavily covered; advanced scheduling policy remains outstanding.

completed.label: Appointment read and lifecycle
completed.detail: Future appointment search/detail plus create, cancel, delete, reschedule, arrival, check-out, no-show, category, pending status, comments, provider, facility, and billing-location edits are implemented.

completed.label: Recurring appointment behavior
completed.detail: Recurring metadata, generated series expansion, exception dates, occurrence cancel/restore/reschedule, recurring root propagation, monthly/day/workday/year units, days-of-week, monthly repeat-on, and series recurrence update are covered.

completed.label: Overlap and reminder readiness
completed.detail: Provider, patient, and room overlap tolerance plus seeded reminder due/channel/contact derivation are covered.

completed.label: Appointment protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now requires an active session, the Calendar page gates appointment search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy schedule-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Appointment authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now enforces mirrored Appointment ACL access, the seeded front-desk session can retrieve appointment schedules through its Front Office membership, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Appointment mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now distinguishes Appointment view access from write-level create, update, status, recurrence restore, occurrence reschedule, and deletion operations.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment list readiness
completed.detail: Signed-in portal patients can see the same future and past appointment card facts that legacy OpenEMR exposes from the portal home, with parity anchored to MOD-PAT-0004.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment request readiness
completed.detail: Signed-in portal patients can request a pending appointment and generate the same provider-facing reminder note that legacy OpenEMR records for portal scheduling requests.

completed.label: Patient portal appointment request options readiness
completed.detail: Signed-in portal patients now receive request-form visit categories, provider choices, facility choices, duration defaults, and patient-specific default provider/facility selections from target data.

outstanding: Stricter provider and facility availability validation, resource conflict enforcement beyond tolerance/readiness reporting, and waitlist flows.
Reminder delivery, delivery audit, templates, and appointment-to-encounter or appointment-to-billing conversion.
deferred: Category administration beyond seeded categories and richer scheduling policy configuration.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Scheduling is functionally important and broad, but the implemented slice set already covers much of its core behavior.
areas.estimateRationale: Appointment lifecycle, portal appointment lists, patient-requested pending appointments, request-form options/defaults, recurrence, overlap, reminder-readiness, protected schedule access, front-desk ACL-authorized access, and mutation-specific Appointment write enforcement are broad; advanced availability, waitlists, and delivery/audit remain.

areas.name: Encounters And Clinical Notes
areas.summary: Encounter read/write, protected access, ACL-backed authorization-policy enforcement, mutation-specific authorization-policy readiness, SOAP/vitals, sign-off, co-signature, amendment history, metadata, billing/claim/procedure/diagnosis linkage, and encounter-scoped document actions are represented, with advanced clinical workflow policy still outstanding.

completed.label: Encounter lifecycle and clinical note facts
completed.detail: Encounter search/detail, vitals, SOAP create/update/delete, metadata mutation, sign-off, and co-signature readiness are covered.

completed.label: Encounter amendment history readiness
completed.detail: Encounter signature amendment text is now projected as ordered amendment history, blank sign-off notes are filtered, and the modernized API/UI render the same history proven against legacy e-sign rows.

completed.label: Encounter billing, claim, procedure, and diagnosis linkage
completed.detail: Read-only linkages and focused mutation paths for billing, diagnoses, fee-sheet entry, procedure-order entry, and procedure-result entry are covered.

completed.label: Encounter-scoped documents
completed.detail: Encounter attachment visibility, upload, binary upload, sign-off, denial, metadata refiling, movement, content replacement, archive/restore, lifecycle timeline, external link, revision, scanned attachment, and binary replacement are covered.

completed.label: Encounter protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized encounter API now requires an active session, the Encounters page gates encounter search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy encounter-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Encounter authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized encounter API now enforces mirrored Authorize Any Encounter access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Encounter mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized encounter mutation API now requires Authorize Any Encounter write access for core encounter create, update, sign, clinical-note, vitals, signature, and delete operations while a temporary Clinicians view-only grant proves read-only denial.

outstanding: Encounter templates, multi-form encounter packages, and amendment policy controls beyond signature-derived history.
Code search, coding validation, claim scrubbing, charge-capture expansion, and comprehensive audit-log export.
deferred: Direct scanner integration and full historical encounter document version chains.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Encounters combine clinical notes, documents, billing links, diagnoses, procedure links, sign-off, and policy-heavy clinical behavior.
areas.estimateRationale: Core encounter, protected encounter access, ACL-backed encounter authorization, core mutation-specific encounter authorization, SOAP/vitals, sign-off, amendment-history projection, document, billing, diagnosis, and procedure linkages are covered; templates and deeper policy controls remain.

areas.name: Clinical Lists, Medications, And Prescriptions
areas.summary: Problem, allergy, medication-list, prescription, patient-portal clinical summary including problem, allergy, and medication date columns, ended problem/allergy/medication-list rows, prescription modified dates, date-added start timestamps, and active end-date filtering, and immunization basics are parity-covered behind protected and ACL-authorized clinical-list access, while clinical vocabulary and prescribing depth remain future scope.

completed.label: Clinical list read model
completed.detail: Problems, allergies, medication list entries, and prescriptions render through the modernized Lists module and shared parity plan.

completed.label: Focused clinical list mutations
completed.detail: Allergy, problem-list, medication-list, prescription, and immunization lifecycle behaviors are covered.

completed.label: Clinical list protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized clinical-list API now requires an active session, the Lists page gates chart-list data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy chart-list protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Clinical list authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized clinical-list API now enforces mirrored Medical/History access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient portal clinical summary readiness
completed.detail: Signed-in portal patients can see the same problem date columns, ended problem-list rows, allergy date/referrer columns, ended allergy rows, medication-list date columns, ended medication-list rows, active-prescription, active-prescription modified-date, prescription date-added/start-date, and active-prescription end-date filtering facts that legacy OpenEMR exposes through the portal clinical-list pages.

outstanding: Vocabulary lookup, duplicate detection, diagnosis interactions, medication reconciliation, structured dose/frequency fields, and audit history.
Pharmacy routing, refill requests, controlled-substance rules, and broader e-prescribing workflow.
deferred: External formulary, pharmacy, and vaccine inventory integrations.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Clinical lists and prescribing are important, but narrower than encounters, labs, billing, documents, and production readiness in this modernization scope.
areas.estimateRationale: List and focused mutation paths now sit behind protected session access and the first clinical-list ACL gate, and patient portal clinical-summary projection now includes problem, allergy, and medication date-column/ended-row fidelity plus active prescription modified-date, date-added/start-date, and end-date filtering fidelity; vocabulary lookup, medication reconciliation, pharmacy routing, and prescribing controls remain.

areas.name: Messaging And Portal-Facing Data
areas.summary: Patient message visibility, lifecycle, assignment, content editing, basic reply threading, portal metadata preservation, pnotes-compatible update metadata stamping, portal inbox/compose/reply/thread-view/archive/read-status/mark-all-read/batch-archive/all-folder/deleted-folder/pagination and forward-to-practice behavior, protected message access, ACL-authorized patient-notes access, and mutation-specific Patient Notes authorization are covered; richer communication workflows remain open.

completed.label: Patient messages and portal-enabled state
completed.detail: Portal flag and patient-message read behavior are covered through the Messages module.

completed.label: Message lifecycle, assignment, content, and reply
completed.detail: Create, close, soft-delete, hard-delete, reassignment, title/body edit, and pnotes-compatible reply append behavior are covered.

completed.label: Portal metadata preservation
completed.detail: Seeded pnotes portal relation and plaintext encryption flags are mirrored into the modernized message schema, API, UI, and parity probes.

completed.label: Message update metadata stamping
completed.detail: Patient-message edits now preserve pnotes-compatible update_by/update_date behavior through modernized schema, API, UI, parity probes, and shared mutation tests.

completed.label: Message protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message API now requires an active session, the Messages page gates patient-message search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy patient-notes protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Message authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message API now enforces mirrored Patient Notes access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Message mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message mutation API now requires Patient Notes add-only for message creation and Patient Notes write for status, content, assignment, reply, archive, and delete changes.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message inbox, compose, recipient directory, subject presets, HTML body rendering, pagination, reply, thread view, archive, read status, mark-all-read, batch archive, All folder, and Deleted folder
completed.detail: The signed-in patient portal can render inbox messages, preserve raw secure-message HTML at the data/workflow boundary, render allowed formatting while stripping links and images at the UI boundary, page message folders with the OpenEMR-compatible 20-message page size, load the OpenEMR-compatible recipient directory with admin fallback routing, load OpenEMR-compatible compose subject presets, compose sent and practice copies through the selected route, reply to seeded inbox messages with reply_mail_chain linkage, view chronological conversation threads, archive active messages through legacy soft-delete semantics, mark active messages read while preserving inbox visibility, mirror the legacy toolbar mark-all-read browser-only status change, archive selected messages together, view the active All-folder mailbox projection, and inspect owner-scoped archived rows in the Deleted folder.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message lifecycle audit readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal now records compose, reply, read-status, single-message archive, and selected-message archive audit events, exposes a session-protected message-audit endpoint, renders a Message Audit timeline in the Portal Secure Messages panel, and parity compares normalized legacy source-action evidence with stored modernized audit history.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message encrypted-body readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal now protects encrypted secure-message bodies at the API boundary, renders a fixed protected-body placeholder in the Portal Secure Messages panel, and parity verifies the behavior with cleanup-backed encrypted mailbox rows on both targets.

completed.label: Patient portal secure-message forward-to-practice readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient portal can forward an inbound secure message into the practice-side patient-message queue, update the portal message status to Sent, and render the forward control while parity verifies cleanup-backed forwarding on both targets.

outstanding: Full encryption key-management/decryption policy, attachments, richer routing queues beyond the recipient directory and basic forward-to-practice, priority and escalation behavior, notification delivery, and broader audit reporting policy.
deferred: Patient portal account provisioning and external notification providers.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Messaging is a smaller domain in the current target, with most remaining depth around portal communications and notifications.
areas.estimateRationale: Message visibility, lifecycle, basic reply append behavior, portal metadata preservation, update metadata stamping, portal inbox/compose/recipient-directory/subject-preset/HTML-body rendering/pagination/reply/thread-view/archive/read-status/mark-all-read/batch-archive/all-folder/deleted-folder and forward-to-practice behavior, secure-message lifecycle audit, encrypted-body protection at the portal API/UI boundary, protected message access, ACL-backed Patient Notes authorization, and mutation-specific add/write Patient Notes policy enforcement are covered; full encryption key management, attachments, richer routing queues, notifications, and broader audit reporting policy remain substantial.

areas.name: Billing, AR, Statements, And Collections
areas.summary: Fee sheet, diagnosis/modifier/correction, claim-status, payment-posting, patient-payment, account rollups, statements, batches, collections basics, protected billing access, ACL-authorized Billing access, and Billing write mutation policy are represented; full revenue-cycle processing remains a large tail.

completed.label: Fee sheet and billing mutations
completed.detail: Read-only fee-sheet billing plus CPT creation, diagnosis coding, correction, and modifier workflows are covered.

completed.label: Claims, payments, ledger, and balances
completed.detail: Claim status, payment posting, patient payment capture, account balance, aging, ledger, and statement readiness are covered.

completed.label: Statements, batches, and collections
completed.detail: Statement generation, statement PDF export, batch candidate/package export, collections work queue, and follow-up task lifecycle are covered.

completed.label: Billing protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing API group now requires an active session, the Fees page gates fee sheet, statement, batch, collections, and revenue-cycle mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy fee-sheet protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Billing authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing API now enforces mirrored Billing access, accepts admin sessions through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Billing mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing mutation API now keeps fee-sheet and revenue-cycle reads on Billing view access while requiring Billing write for billing-line, claim, payment, and collections follow-up mutations.

outstanding: Claim generation depth, payer adjudication, ERA/EOB import, remittance workflows, refunds/reversals beyond simple voiding, receipt printing, and statement delivery.
Audit history, charge templates, diagnosis pointer validation, modifier validation, and richer revenue-cycle exception handling.
deferred: Production clearinghouse, payer, and payment-provider integrations.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Revenue-cycle behavior has a large state space across fees, claims, payments, statements, collections, exceptions, and integrations.
areas.estimateRationale: Fee-sheet, claims status, payments, statements, batches, collections basics, protected billing access, ACL-backed Billing authorization, and mutation-specific Billing write enforcement are covered; full revenue-cycle processing remains large.

areas.name: Documents And Attachments
areas.summary: Patient and encounter document read, upload, retrieval, preview, sign-off, denial, metadata, archive, replacement, revision, lifecycle, scanned-readiness, external-link behavior, patient portal list/download, protected document access, ACL-authorized document access, and mutation-specific document authorization are broadly covered; production document operations remain open.

completed.label: Patient document read, retrieval, and mutation
completed.detail: Document metadata, text content retrieval/download, text and binary upload, sign-off, denial, metadata refiling, archive/restore, content replacement, replacement revision, binary replacement, and external-link filing are covered.

completed.label: Preview, thumbnail, lifecycle, and scanned-readiness
completed.detail: Document preview readiness, current revision facts, image inline preview, image thumbnail, PDF inline preview, lifecycle timeline, and scanned attachment readiness are covered.

completed.label: Document protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient document API now requires an active session, the Documents page gates document search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy document-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Patient document authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient document API now enforces mirrored Documents ACL access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient document mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient and encounter document mutation APIs now separate Documents add-only filing from write-level metadata/content/review/archive operations and Documents Delete hard-delete authority.

completed.label: Patient portal document list/download readiness
completed.detail: The authenticated patient portal can render active patient-owned document categories and create a selected-document ZIP package with OpenEMR-compatible ownership, deleted-row, and non-downloadable external-link filtering.

outstanding: Direct scanner capture, OCR execution and queueing, generated PDF thumbnails, document routing queues, full historical version rows, encryption/key management, and retention policy.
External object storage, CCDA import/export, broader patient-portal document access rules beyond active owned list/download, and document delivery integrations.
deferred: Fax/SMS attachments and external storage provider adapters.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Document workflows span storage, previews, reviews, revisions, retention, authorization, scanner/OCR, and patient/encounter contexts.
areas.estimateRationale: Document read, upload, preview, review, archive, replacement, revision, patient portal list/download, protected access, focused ACL-authorized access, and mutation-specific add/write/delete document authorization paths are broad; scanner, OCR, storage, and retention remain.

areas.name: Labs And Procedures
areas.summary: Completed results, pending orders, patient portal lab-results visibility, procedure lifecycle, corrections, specimens, clinical order queues, order transmit readiness, review queues, bulk report sign-off, report review reopen, procedure order catalog seed/read/lifecycle/import behavior, lab-provider catalog/directory/lifecycle/configuration/address-book linkage, protected procedure access, ACL-authorized Lab Results access, and mutation-specific lab add/write/sign authorization are parity-covered; real external lab operations remain open.

completed.label: Procedure order/result lifecycle
completed.detail: Completed result read, pending orders, create/complete/report/result/delete, encounter procedure-order/result entry, result correction, specimen metadata/detail, order correction, report correction, and report sign-off are covered.

completed.label: Review queues and lab providers
completed.detail: Clinical order queue, order transmit readiness, report review queue, patient/date/provider/lab filters, two-report bulk sign-off, signed-report reopen review, lab-provider catalog ownership, provider directory, provider lifecycle, provider transport/configuration fields, and linked address-book organization behavior are covered.

completed.label: Procedure order catalog
completed.detail: Permanent gold-data procedure order catalog rows, provider groups, orderable panel rows, modernized Reports rendering, temporary catalog item create/update/deactivate/delete lifecycle behavior, and PathGroup-style vendor compendium import semantics are covered.

completed.label: Procedure protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure API group now requires an active session, the Procedures page gates procedure results and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy procedure-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Procedure authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure API now enforces mirrored Lab Results access, accepts admin sessions through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, keeps the Procedures access panel available for retry, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Procedure mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure mutation API now separates Lab Results add-only, Lab Results write, and Sign Lab Results authority so clinicians can create order/report records while status updates, deletion, and sign-off require stronger permissions.

completed.label: Patient portal lab results readiness
completed.detail: Signed-in portal patients can see completed lab order, report, and result facts for their own record, with parity against legacy `portal/get_lab_results.php`.

outstanding: Reviewer assignment, queue notifications, result amendment/version history depth, chain-of-custody beyond current specimen fields, and audit-log export.

External SFTP/filesystem/web-service execution, external lab reconciliation, credential encryption/rotation policy, linked-provider deletion policy, and deeper role-specific authorization breadth beyond the first mutation-specific lab policy slice.
deferred: Production external lab ordering interfaces and richer address-book organization lifecycle administration.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Labs and procedures include order/result lifecycle, queues, providers, catalogs, specimens, reports, and future external transport/reconciliation.
areas.estimateRationale: Procedure lifecycle, clinical order queues, order transmit readiness, review queues, bulk report sign-off, report review reopen, patient portal lab-results visibility, providers, configuration, address-book linkage, order catalog reads/lifecycle mutations, focused vendor compendium import, protected procedure access, ACL-backed Lab Results authorization, and add/write/sign mutation policy boundaries are covered; external lab operations and deeper lab policy remain.

areas.name: Administration, Security, And Audit
areas.summary: Facility, user, ACL, demo login-readiness, session-readiness, login-audit basics, focused audit protection, protected administration APIs, ACL-backed administration, operational-report, patient, patient-mutation, clinical-list, appointment, appointment-mutation, encounter, encounter-mutation, document, document-mutation, message, message-mutation, billing, procedure, and procedure-mutation authorization-policy enforcement, protected operational reports, protected patient APIs, protected clinical-list APIs, protected appointment APIs, protected encounter APIs, protected document APIs, protected message APIs, protected billing APIs, and protected procedure APIs are covered, but production identity, MFA, broader policy depth, and broader audit remain major outstanding scope.

completed.label: Administration directory and lifecycle basics
completed.detail: Users/facilities directory behavior, facility lifecycle, and user lifecycle are implemented.

completed.label: Focused access-control behavior
completed.detail: Default ACL matrix, focused access-permission grant/revoke, and user group membership mutation are covered.

completed.label: Admin login readiness
completed.detail: The modernized app seeds a hashed demo admin credential, exposes a login-readiness API, renders an Admin-page verification panel, and compares login success and invalid-password rejection with legacy.

completed.label: Admin login audit readiness
completed.detail: Successful and failed admin login attempts are recorded in a modernized audit table, surfaced through an API and Admin-page audit panel, and compared with legacy OpenEMR login log rows.

completed.label: Admin session readiness
completed.detail: Successful admin login issues a modernized session, current-session validation and logout endpoints are exposed, the Admin page renders session status, and parity compares legacy cookie logout behavior with the modernized session lifecycle.

completed.label: Admin audit protection readiness
completed.detail: The login audit endpoint now requires an active admin session, the Admin page only loads audit events after sign-in, and parity compares legacy log-viewer protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Admin directory protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized administration API group now requires an active admin session, the Admin page loads user/facility/ACL data only after sign-in, and parity compares legacy admin-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Administration authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized administration API group now enforces the mirrored ACL Administration permission, accepts the admin session, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Operational reports authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized operational reports API and CSV export now enforce mirrored Patient Report access, accept the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, reject an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient chart authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient search/chart API now enforces mirrored Demographics access, accepts admin and front-desk sessions through seeded write grants, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient mutation API now separates Demographics add-only registration access from write-level contact, demographics, insurance, and deletion authority.

completed.label: Clinical list authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized clinical-list API now enforces mirrored Medical/History access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Appointment authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now enforces mirrored Appointment access, seeds the front-desk demo account into the Front Office ACL group, accepts that session for calendar retrieval, and parity compares the policy with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Appointment mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now requires Appointment write access for schedule create, update, status, recurrence restore, occurrence reschedule, and deletion mutations while preserving view-only schedule reads.

completed.label: Encounter authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized encounter API now enforces mirrored Authorize Any Encounter access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Encounter mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized core encounter mutation API now requires Authorize Any Encounter write access and parity proves a Clinicians view-only grant can read encounters while create, update, sign, and delete operations remain blocked.

completed.label: Patient document authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized document API now enforces mirrored Documents access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient document mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized document mutation API now requires Documents add-only for document filing, Documents write for metadata/content/review/archive changes, and Documents Delete write for hard deletion.

completed.label: Patient message authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message API now enforces mirrored Patient Notes access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Patient message mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message mutation API now requires Patient Notes add-only for clinician-created notes and Patient Notes write for status, content, assignment, reply, archive, and delete operations.

completed.label: Billing authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing API now enforces mirrored Billing access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Billing mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing mutation API now requires Billing write for fee-sheet, claim, payment, and collections follow-up mutations while Billing view remains sufficient for read-only revenue-cycle data.

completed.label: Procedure authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure API now enforces mirrored Lab Results access, accepts the admin session through the OpenEMR return-value hierarchy, rejects an authenticated front-desk session with 403, and parity compares that behavior with the legacy ACL matrix.

completed.label: Procedure mutation authorization policy readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure mutation API now requires Lab Results add-only for clinician-created order/report records, Lab Results write for status/delete updates, and Sign Lab Results write for report sign-off.

completed.label: Operational reports protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized operational reports API and CSV export now require an active session, the Reports page gates operational report data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy report-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Patient chart protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized patient search/chart API now requires an active session, the Patient/Client page gates patient data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy patient-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Clinical list protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized clinical-list API now requires an active session, the Lists page gates chart-list data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy clinical-list protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Appointment protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized appointment API now requires an active session, the Calendar page gates schedule search and appointment mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy schedule-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Encounter protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized encounter API group now requires an active session, the Encounters page gates encounter search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy encounter-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Document protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized document API group now requires an active session, the Documents page gates document search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy document-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Message protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized message API group now requires an active session, the Messages page gates patient-message search and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy patient-notes protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Billing protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized billing API group now requires an active session, the Fees page gates fee-sheet and revenue-cycle data until sign-in, and parity compares legacy fee-sheet protection with modernized API/UI protection.

completed.label: Procedure protection readiness
completed.detail: The modernized procedure API group now requires an active session, the Procedures page gates procedure results and mutations until sign-in, and parity compares legacy procedure-page protection with modernized API/UI protection.

outstanding: ASP.NET Core Identity, password lifecycle, MFA, user-facility restriction matrices, broader role/permission management, and policy enforcement beyond the focused administration, operational-report, patient, patient-mutation, clinical-list, appointment, appointment-mutation, encounter, encounter-mutation, document, document-mutation, message, message-mutation, billing, billing-mutation, procedure, and procedure mutation ACL gates.
Comprehensive audit-event logging beyond login attempts and audit export across clinical, billing, document, lab, and admin workflows.
deferred: Production identity-provider integration and security hardening beyond local parity scope.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Administration, authentication, authorization, security policy, facility restriction, and audit are cross-cutting and high-risk.
areas.estimateRationale: Directory, lifecycle, ACL, group, demo login readiness, session readiness, login audit basics, focused login-audit protection, protected administration APIs, ACL-backed administration, operational-report, patient, patient-mutation, clinical-list, appointment, appointment-mutation, encounter, encounter-mutation, document, document-mutation, message, message-mutation, billing, billing-mutation, procedure, and procedure mutation authorization policy enforcement, protected operational reports, protected patient chart APIs, protected clinical-list APIs, protected appointment APIs, protected encounter APIs, protected document APIs, protected message APIs, protected billing APIs, and protected procedure APIs exist; Identity, MFA, broader policy depth, facility restrictions, and broader audit remain major scope.

areas.name: Reports, Workbench, And Evidence
areas.summary: Operational reports, CSV export, Workbench orchestration, test management, comparison artifacts, drill-ins, safe artifact links, and direct run/report links are implemented; richer evidence exploration remains future scope.

completed.label: Operational reporting
completed.detail: Gold-dataset operational reports and normalized CSV export behavior are covered.

completed.label: Workbench orchestration and evidence
completed.detail: Managed lifecycle controls, seed actions, native tests, parity plans, custom parity runs, side-by-side comparisons, drill-ins, artifact links, direct run/report evidence links, timeline, architecture, and progress views are implemented.

outstanding: Screenshot thumbnails, normalized probe detail views, accepted-difference tracking, reliability trends, and historical progress charts.
Saved report definitions and richer downloadable report formats.
deferred: Future CI integration and long-term evidence retention policy.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Workbench evidence is strategically important but smaller than the clinical, billing, document, lab, and production modernization domains.
areas.estimateRationale: Operational reporting, Workbench orchestration, comparisons, drill-ins, direct report links, timeline, architecture, and progress views are substantial; screenshots, trends, and report depth remain.

areas.name: Integrations, Migration, And Production Readiness
areas.summary: The current project is strong as a local parity modernization workbench, but production migration and external integration readiness remain intentionally outside the completed slice set.

completed.label: Deterministic synthetic migration contract
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset maps to both legacy MariaDB and modernized PostgreSQL for implemented workflows.

outstanding: Legacy data extraction from real OpenEMR instances, migration rehearsal tooling, rollback strategy, production deployment, CI/CD, observability, backup/restore, and security review.
External integrations for labs, e-prescribing, vaccine registries, portal, fax/SMS, CCDA/export, clearinghouse, payment providers, and document storage.
deferred: PHI-bearing test strategy and compliance validation; this repository should continue using synthetic non-PHI data.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Migration and production readiness are large remaining concerns, but currently bounded as later-stage scope outside the local parity loop.
areas.estimateRationale: The synthetic migration contract exists; real extraction, migration rehearsal, deployment, observability, CI/CD, backup, and external integrations remain.
